message.content:
## Source Extractor

Research question: "Competitive landscape and market analysis for AI-powered recruitment platforms targeting solo/independent recruiters and small recruiting agencies (teams of 5-10), with a freemium model (free individual tier + paid team tier).

Research these dimensions specifically:
1. Existing recruitment platforms (ATS, recruiting CRM, applicant tracking) that target solo recruiters and small agencies — e.g. Recruit CRM, Loxo, Crelate, Manatal, JobAdder, Zoho Recruit, Bullhorn, RecruiterFlow, Vincere, iSmartRecruit, Ceipal, Tracker, Hireflow, hiring tools like Workable, Ashby, Greenhouse (note which are NOT for small/solo). For each relevant one: target customer, pricing/tiers, free tier availability, AI features, key strengths/weaknesses.
2. Which of these use a freemium or free-tier model (Zoho Recruit free plan, etc.) and how they monetize. How "easy to start" / self-serve onboarding is positioned.
3. Where AI is being applied in recruiting today (resume parsing, candidate matching/ranking, sourcing, outreach email generation, interview scheduling, candidate Q&A, job description writing, chrome extensions for sourcing from LinkedIn). Which features are table-stakes vs differentiators in 2025-2026.
4. The pain points of small/independent recruiters: data scattered across WhatsApp/Excel/Google Drive, time spent on admin, lack of context on past placements, no revenue visibility. What tools claim to solve these and gaps that remain.
5. Pricing benchmarks: typical per-seat/month pricing for recruiting CRM/ATS at the small-agency level, and what free tiers include.
6. Market size and trends for recruitment software / recruiting CRM, especially the SMB / independent recruiter segment."

Fetch and extract key claims from this source:
**URL:** https://www.selectsoftwarereviews.com/blog/recruitment-software-pricing
**Title:** Recruitment Software Pricing Guide (Update for 2026) — SSR (SelectSoftwareReviews)
**Found via:** pricing benchmarks & market size search

## Task
1. Use WebFetch to retrieve the page content.
2. Assess source quality: primary research/institution? secondary reporting? blog/opinion? forum? unreliable?
3. Extract 2-5 FALSIFIABLE claims that bear on the research question. Each claim must:
   - be a concrete, checkable statement (not vague generalities)
   - include a direct quote from the source as support
   - be rated central/supporting/tangential to the research question
4. Note publish date if available.

If the fetch fails or the page is irrelevant/paywalled, return claims: [] and sourceQuality: "unreliable".

Structured output only.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll fetch the page content first.

input.prompt: Extract all concrete, checkable facts about recruitment software pricing: specific dollar amounts per seat/month, free tier availability and what they include, pricing tiers for named platforms (Recruit CRM, Loxo, Crelate, Manatal, JobAdder, Zoho Recruit, Bullhorn, RecruiterFlow, Vincere, Workable, Ashby, Greenhouse, etc.), target customer segments (solo recruiters, small agencies), AI features, freemium models, and any market size/trend statistics. Also note the publish date or last-updated date of the article.

content.content:
# Recruitment Software Pricing: Concrete Facts

**Publication Date:** April 17, 2026 (updated for 2026)

## Specific Pricing by Platform

| Platform | Starting Price | Free Plan? | Free Trial? |
|----------|---|---|---|
| Manatal | $15/mo | No | Yes |
| Zoho Recruit | $25/mo | Yes | Yes |
| Recruit CRM | $40-85/mo* | Yes | Yes |
| JazzHR | $75/mo | No | Yes |
| BambooHR | $99/mo (up to 20 employees) | No | Yes |
| SmartRecruiters | $100/mo | No | No |
| ICIMS | $109/mo | No | No |
| Loxo | $119/mo | Yes | Yes |
| Breezy HR | $157/mo | Yes | Yes |
| Workable | $299/mo | No | Yes |
| Recruitee | €301/mo | No | Yes |
| Trakstar Hire | $299/mo | No | No |
| Gem | $99-135/mo* | No | No |
| Lever | $300/mo (annual required) | No | No |
| Teamtailor | $300/mo (under 25 employees) | No | Yes |
| Ashby | $300/mo (up to 10 employees) | No | No |
| SeekOut | $350/mo (annual required) | No | No |
| Greenhouse | $500/mo (annual required) | No | No |
| hireEZ | $500/mo (annual required) | No | No |
| Fetcher | $379/mo (annual required) | No | No |
| Pinpoint | $384/mo (annual required) | No | No |
| VidCruiter | $1,000/mo | No | No |

*Recruit CRM: $40/mo emerging markets; $85/mo US & Europe. Gem: $135/mo non-staffing; $99/mo staffing firms.

## Pricing by Business Size

- **Small businesses (under 50 employees):** $15–$200/user/month
- **Mid-sized (50–249 employees):** $150–$400/user/month
- **Large (250–999 employees):** $400–$1,000+/user/month
- **Enterprise (1,000+ employees):** Custom negotiated pricing

## Pricing Models Identified

The document describes six primary models: subscription-based, pay-per-user, tiered, custom, freemium, and open-source.

## Additional Costs Beyond Base Subscription

- Implementation/onboarding fees
- Customization services
- API integrations (e.g., PCRecruiter: $50/month)
- Data storage overages (e.g., Zoho: $4.60/month per 5GB; $11.50 for backup)
- Extra features/add-ons
- Support upgrades (e.g., JazzHR dedicated account manager: $119/month add-on)
- Data migration services
- Overage charges (e.g., Wild Noodle: $20 per extra test)
- Third-party service integrations

## Notable Trend: Price Increases

Recruitee pricing history cited: $185/month (Q4 2022) → $199/month (2024) → €270/month (2025) → €301/month (January 2026).

## Market Insights

The document notes that "few vendors have transparent pricing" and most offerings are "tiered so that the features you really want cost extra."

content.text: The fetch succeeded with rich pricing data. This is a software review aggregator site (secondary source).

claims.claim: Zoho Recruit and Recruit CRM offer free plans, while most mid-to-enterprise ATS platforms (Workable, Ashby, Greenhouse, Lever, Teamtailor) do not — Zoho Recruit starts at $25/mo, Recruit CRM at $40-85/mo, and both also offer free trials.
claims.quote: Zoho Recruit | $25/mo | Yes | Yes ... Recruit CRM | $40-85/mo | Yes | Yes

claims.claim: Recruiting software pricing for small businesses (under 50 employees) ranges roughly $15-$200 per user per month, with Manatal the cheapest entry at $15/mo and Loxo at $119/mo.

Several platforms positioned as higher-end (Ashby, Greenhouse, Lever, Teamtailor) start around $300-$500/mo and require annual commitments, with no free plan, marking them as not aimed at solo/small-agency self-serve buyers.
claims.quote: Ashby | $300/mo (up to 10 employees) | No ... Greenhouse | $500/mo (annual required) | No ... Lever | $300/mo (annual required) | No

claims.claim: Freemium is one of six recognized pricing models in recruitment software, alongside subscription, pay-per-user, tiered, custom, and open-source; free tiers exist among lower-cost ATS/CRM tools (Zoho Recruit, Recruit CRM, Loxo, Breezy HR).
claims.quote: The document describes six primary models: subscription-based, pay-per-user, tiered, custom, freemium, and open-source.

claims.claim: Recruiting software vendors generally lack transparent pricing and use tiered structures where desired features cost extra, plus additional fees for implementation, integrations, storage overages, and support — e.g., Zoho data storage at $4.60/mo per 5GB and JazzHR dedicated account manager at $119/mo add-on.
claims.quote: few vendors have transparent pricing ... tiered so that the features you really want cost extra

content.text: I've extracted the key claims from the SelectSoftwareReviews recruitment software pricing guide. The source is a secondary software-review aggregator with detailed, falsifiable per-platform pricing data directly relevant to the freemium and small-agency pricing dimensions of the research question.
